---
title: "Test Merge House Price Data"
author: "Jacopo Olivieri"
date: "2024-01-31"
output:
  html_document:
    fig_width: 6
    fig_aspect_ratio: 0.618
knitr:
  opts_chunk:
    echo: false
    warning: false
    message: false
    fig.align: "center"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Initialize packages with version control
if (!requireNamespace("renv", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("renv")
}

# Run manual renv commands if needed
# renv::activate() # Activate the project library # nolint
# renv::restore()  # Restore the environment if running for first time # nolint
# renv::snapshot()  # After adding/updating packages, snapshot the state # nolint


# Load required packages
required_packages <- c(
  "rmarkdown", "rio", "tidyverse", "purrr", "here",
  "janitor", "data.table", "lubridate", "rnrfa", "sf",
  "furrr", "future", "tmap"
)
install_if_missing <- function(packages) {
  new_packages <- packages[!sapply(packages, requireNamespace, quietly = TRUE)]
  if (length(new_packages) > 0) {
    install.packages(new_packages)
  }
  invisible(sapply(packages, library, character.only = TRUE))
}
install_if_missing(required_packages)

# set up parallel processing
setup_parallel <- function() {
  n_workers <- parallelly::availableCores() - 1
  machine_ram <- memuse::Sys.meminfo()$totalram
  ram_limit <- as.numeric(0.7 * machine_ram)

  options(future.globals.maxSize = ram_limit)
  future::plan(future::multisession, workers = n_workers)
  logger::log_info("Parallel processing initialized with {n_workers} workers")
}
setup_parallel()


# Initialize logging
log_path <- here::here("output", "log", "test_merge_house_price_data.log")
if (!dir.exists(dirname(log_path))) {
  dir.create(dirname(log_path), recursive = TRUE)
}
logger::log_appender(logger::appender_file(log_path))
logger::log_layout(logger::layout_glue_colors)
```

## Load Data

### Load EDM Data

```{r, load_data}
dat <- import(here(
  "data", "processed",
  "individual_edm_location.RDATA"
), trust = TRUE)

dat_yr <- dat$yearly
dat_mo <- dat$monthly

spill_sites <- import(here("data", "processed", "unique_spill_sites.rds"),
  trust = TRUE
)
```

### Load House Price Data

```{r}
house_price_data <- import(here("data", "processed", "house_price.rds"),
  trust = TRUE
)
```

### Load Lookup

```{r}
spill_lookup <- import(here("data", "processed", "spill_house_lookup.rds"),
  trust = TRUE
)
```

### Plot London Spill Sites

```{r}
# Define a London bounding box (WGS84 coordinates)
london_bbox <- st_as_sfc(
  st_bbox(c(xmin = -0.5103, ymin = 51.2868, xmax = 0.3340, ymax = 51.6919), 
          crs = st_crs(4326))
)

# Convert spill_sites to an sf object.
# Note: spill_sites has "easting" and "northing" in British National Grid (EPSG:27700),
# so we convert and then transform to WGS84.
spill_sites_sf <- st_as_sf(
  spill_sites, 
  coords = c("easting", "northing"), 
  crs = 27700) %>%
  st_transform(crs = 4326)

# Filter spill sites that fall within London
london_spill_sites <- spill_sites_sf[st_within(spill_sites_sf, london_bbox, sparse = FALSE), ]

# Select a random sample of 10 spill sites in London
set.seed(123)
sampled_spill_sites <- london_spill_sites %>% slice_sample(n = 10)

# Convert house_price_data to an sf object using longitude and latitude (WGS84)
house_sf <- st_as_sf(house_price_data, coords = c("longitude", "latitude"), crs = 4326)

# Create 10 km buffers around the sampled spill sites
spill_buffers <- st_buffer(sampled_spill_sites, dist = 10000)

# Identify houses within the union of these buffers
valid_spill_buffers <- st_make_valid(spill_buffers)
houses_in_buffer <- st_intersection(house_sf, st_union(valid_spill_buffers))


# Plot with tmap in interactive mode
tmap_mode("view")
map <- tm_shape(spill_buffers) +
  tm_polygons(col = "lightgreen", alpha = 0.3, border.alpha = 0) +
  tm_shape(houses_in_buffer) +
  tm_dots(col = "darkblue", size = 0.5, alpha = 0.8) +
  tm_shape(sampled_spill_sites) +
  tm_dots(col = "red", size = 1, alpha = 0.9) +
  tm_layout(
    title = "House Sales within 10km of Sampled Spill Sites in London",
    legend.outside = TRUE
  )
map
```




```{r}
# Filter to London house sales and associated spill sites
london_house_ids <- house_price_data %>%
  filter(region == "London") %>%
  pull(house_id)

london_spill_ids <- spill_lookup %>%
  filter(house_id %in% london_house_ids) %>%
  distinct(site_id) %>%
  pull(site_id)

london_spill_sites <- spill_sites %>%
  filter(site_id %in% london_spill_ids)

# Randomly sample 10 spill sites
set.seed(123) # For reproducibility
sampled_sites <- london_spill_sites %>%
  slice_sample(n = 3)

# Get houses within 10km of sampled sites
sampled_house_ids <- spill_lookup %>%
  filter(site_id %in% sampled_sites$site_id) %>%
  distinct(house_id) %>%
  pull(house_id)

matched_houses_sample <- house_price_data %>%
  filter(house_id %in% sampled_house_ids) %>% 
  slice_sample(prop = .10)

# Convert to spatial objects (EPSG 27700: British National Grid)
sampled_sites_sf <- st_as_sf(sampled_sites, coords = c("easting", "northing"), crs = 27700)
sampled_houses_sf <- st_as_sf(matched_houses_sample, coords = c("easting", "northing"), crs = 27700) 

# Create 10km buffers around spill sites
sampled_buffers <- st_buffer(sampled_sites_sf, dist = 10000)

# Transform to WGS84 for visualization
sampled_sites_sf <- st_transform(sampled_sites_sf, 4326)
sampled_buffers <- st_transform(sampled_buffers, 4326)
sampled_houses_sf <- st_transform(sampled_houses_sf, 4326)

# London boundary
london_boundary_sf <- ne_states(country = "United Kingdom", 
                                returnclass = "sf") %>%
  filter(region == "Greater London")



# Generate interactive map
tmap_mode("view")
spill_map <- tm_shape(sampled_buffers) +
  tm_polygons(fill = "lightgreen", 
              fill_alpha = 0.3, 
              col_alpha = 0) +
  tm_shape(sampled_houses_sf) +
  tm_bubbles(
    size = "price",
    fill = "price",
    fill.scale = tm_scale_intervals(
      values = "brewer.blues",
      n = 4
    ),
    size.scale = tm_scale_continuous(
      values.scale = 3
    ),
    size.legend = tm_legend_hide(),
    fill.legend = tm_legend(
      title = "Price Paid (?)"
    )
  ) +
  tm_shape(sampled_sites_sf) +
    tm_symbols(
      fill = "darkred",
      shape = 18,
      size = 0.7,
      fill_alpha = 0.9,
      fill.legend = tm_legend(title = "Spill Sites")
    ) +
  tm_shape(london_boundary_sf) +
  tm_borders(col = "blue4", lwd = 1) +
  tm_title("London Spill Sites with 10km Buffers and Nearby House Sales") +
  tm_layout(legend.outside = TRUE)

spill_map
```
